step_1_context.text: By next December, I will have graduated from university. Then I can start looking for a job.

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: Use future perfect for actions that will be completed before a specific time in the future.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: Always use a time marker like 'by', 'by the time', 'before' to show the deadline.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: By 6 PM, she ___ cooking dinner.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Future perfect: will have + past participle. 'By 6 PM' sets the deadline.

step_3_interactive_exercises.sentence: By the time you arrive, we ___ (eat) already.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Action (eating) will be complete before the arrival. Use will have + past participle.
